title: Minnie Mouse and the Long-Legged Lego Duck
summary: Mickey, Minnie, and Mole visit Nana Mole's house, which sits at the edge of a vast field of wildflowers. While relaxing on the porch with hibiscus tea, Mole introduces his friends to a bag of magic Legos that can turn into real objects for a short time. Using the magic word 'Aska-baska-noona-maska,' Mickey transforms a Lego flower into a real, fragrant blossom. Minnie then constructs an exceptionally tall Lego duck and brings it to life with the incantation 'Up-sa-fra.' Despite its clumsy, long legs, the real duck runs at incredible speeds through the flower fields before finally returning to its Lego form.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What's the story about Mickey and Minnie and Mole?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. A story about Mickey and Minnie and Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now. Once upon a time, Mickey and Minnie and Mole were visiting Mole's grandmother's house. Now Mole's grandmother, what was her name again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uhh...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did they just call her Nana? Or Nana Mole?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nana Mole!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Nana Mole's house was at the edge of a very big field full of wildflowers and it had a wonderful front porch where they could all sit in the shade and look out at the beautiful sunny day. And Nana Mole had just brought them out each a nice little glass of iced hibiscus tea with a straw, and they were all relaxing on the front porch, Mickey and Minnie and Mole. And then Mole said, 'Hey, I wonder if Nana Mole still has my magic Legos?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mickey said, 'What? Magic Legos? What are those?' And Minnie said, 'I know about Legos, but I don't know about any magic Legos.' And Mole said, 'Yeah, let's ask her.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so a few minutes later, Nana Mole came back out and they asked her about it, and she said, 'Oh, yeah!' And she ran back inside and she got a big bag and she carried it out on the front porch and she dumped it out all over the porch. And you know what was inside?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Magical Legos in all shapes and colors and sizes. And you know what was magical about them?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, just like ordinary Legos, Mole explained, you could use them to make whatever you imagine. But then, if you close your eyes and hold your creation in your hands tight and say a magic word, then pring, your Lego creation turns into something real just for a little while. And so Mole said, 'Do you want to try it?' And what did Mickey and Minnie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did they want to make?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story, then you find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mickey said, 'Hmm, what should I make? Well, when I play with Legos, sometimes I like to make flowers.' And so Mickey got some pieces that looked a little bit like flower petals and he put them all around on another piece all the way around just like the petals of a flower. And then he added another piece to be the stem and he stood it up. And it looked a little bit like a flower, but it was made of Legos. And then Mole said, 'Are you ready?' And Mickey said, 'Ah, I don't know. Is the magic gonna work?' And then Mole said, 'Well, there's only one way to find out, let's just give it a try.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mickey said, 'Okay.' And he picked up the flower he'd made of Lego and he squeezed it between his hands and he said oh, a long funny magic word. What was the long funny magic word that he said, Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, let's make one up.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Aska-baska-noona-maska.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Aska-baska-noona-maska! Well, that's what Mickey said, and then pring, he opened his hands up and there in his hands was a very real fresh flower just like the one he had made out of Lego, but it was real. And he held it to his nose and it smelled like a real flower. And Mole said, 'Now, it's gonna change back in just a minute or two, but you can enjoy it for now.' And then what did Minnie do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She decided to make something too. What did Minnie want to make?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What are some of the things we were making?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A ducky!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A ducky. So Minnie decided to make a little... now did she make a little ducky or a medium ducky or a really big ducky?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A really tall ducky.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A tall ducky. And so Minnie Mouse spent a long time because it took a lot of Legos to make a ducky that was really tall. She stacked them higher and higher to make long legs for the duck. Then she built the body, wings, a little tail and finally a little head with a little orange beak. And she said, 'Quack, quack, here it is!' And Mole said, 'Wow, that's a tall duck. Do you want to try to make it real?' And Minnie said, 'Okay, I'll try.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she squeezed it between her paws and closed her eyes and this time she said a different magic word. What was the magic word she said this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ssss ssss ssss ssss...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was 'Up-sa-fra!' She said. Was that it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mm-hmm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then pring, the very tall Lego duck magically transformed into a very tall real duck! It tried to waddle, and it tipped right over. It said, 'My legs are too long!' Minnie helped the duck stand up. And it said, 'Wow, I may be a long-legged duck that has a hard time standing, but maybe I could run.' And it ran right off the porch and through the field of flowers. Minnie said, 'That's the fastest waddling I've ever seen!' Then it zoomed back and fell over again. And the duck stood up and said, 'Yeah, I'm fine. I had a good time waddling as fast as I could.' And then they heard a funny sound, poof, and the duck turned back into a Lego duck. Now should we do one more?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No? Is that the end of the story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, that's the end of the story.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Mole
characters.description: A resourceful, perpetually humming mole with an impossibly long name, this polite and intrepid mariner serves as a bridge between worlds, translating Frasgle’s unique tongue and guiding the way to the hidden Underground Library. Whether managing rocket controls with wide-eyed curiosity, unraveling treehouse mysteries, or sharing the magic of special Legos, he encourages his companions to embrace the unknown between tending his dandelion yard and baking fragrant banana bread. A lover of both wriggly worms and sweet blueberries, he sings whimsical songs of carnabee sharks and drives a noisy dark brown car, remaining firmly hesitant about boarding giant robots even as he coaxes his friends toward bold new adventures.

characters.name: Nana Mole
characters.description: Mole's grandmother who owns a bag of magical Legos and lives by a field of flowers.

characters.name: The Duck
characters.description: A magical creation with extremely long legs that can waddle at high speeds.

places.name: Nana Mole's House
places.description: A home located at the edge of a large field of wildflowers.

places.name: The Front Porch
places.description: A shady spot where the characters relax, drink hibiscus tea, and play with magic Legos.

places.name: The Field of Wildflowers
places.description: A wide expanse where the magical long-legged duck runs at incredible speeds.
